author: Anna
title: Easy Overnight Sourdough Rolls
ingredients: seeds for topping (e.g. sunflower seeds, sesame seeds, poppy seeds)
seeds for topping (e.g. sunflower seeds, sesame seeds, poppy seeds)

instructions:
Feed the sourdough starter so that it's active and ready to bake by evening. If feeding it in the morning before work, use a ratio of 1:4:4 – in summer, 1:5:5.

Combine all ingredients and knead for 10–15 minutes by hand or, ideally, in a stand mixer.

Leave the dough to rise at room temperature for approximately 3–4 hours, until it has grown by approximately 50 percent. This can happen at varying speeds depending on the room temperature, so keep an eye on your dough.

After the rising time, cover the dough – for example with plastic wrap – and refrigerate overnight (approximately 10–16 hours).

In the morning, spread a mix of seeds on the work surface. Lightly moisten the surface of the dough in the bowl, then turn it out onto the seed mixture. Fold the dough once, then divide into eight pieces.

Bake at 230 °C / 445 °F (convection) for approximately 20 minutes with steam: either using an oven with a steam function, or by placing an oven-safe dish on the oven floor and filling it with hot water just before baking. Alternatively, pour water directly onto the oven floor, as long as your oven can handle it. Tip: After the first 10 minutes, briefly open the oven door to release the steam so the rolls turn crispy.

Here's what your timeline could look like:
Day 1 – 7 am: Feed sourdough starter
Day 1 – 6 pm: Mix the dough
Day 1 – 9 pm: Put the dough in the fridge
Day 2 – 7 am: Bake the rolls

description: Crispy sourdough rolls without the hassle: simple, no stretching or folding, and made overnight. Perfect for busy people and sourdough beginners!
